message:
feat(sharding): Cx-first shard_health_report_with_cx + shard_watchdog_warnings_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close sharding.rs's 2 cx gaps. Both new siblings thread the caller's
cx into each shard's `list_panes` call (via `list_panes_with_cx`),
giving responsive cancellation across multi-shard fleet health
scans.

1. `ShardedWeztermClient::shard_health_report_with_cx(cx)` — two
   checkpoint seams per iteration:
   - Per-shard `cx.checkpoint()?` between iterations (breaks out
     of the loop on cancellation, returning a partial report).
   - `list_panes_with_cx(cx)` replaces `list_panes()` so
     cancellation propagates into the handle's internal IO.

2. `ShardedWeztermClient::shard_watchdog_warnings_with_cx(cx)` —
   pure delegate through `shard_health_report_with_cx`.

Legacy `shard_health_report` + `shard_watchdog_warnings` preserved
unchanged.

Two regression tests pin the contracts:

- `shard_health_report_with_cx_matches_legacy` — 2-shard topology
  (healthy + failing) produces matching overall status, shard
  count, pane counts, and watchdog warning content as the legacy
  path.
- `shard_health_report_with_cx_bails_on_precancelled_cx` —
  pre-cancelled cx produces a report with 0 shards (never touched
  any backend).

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 99/99
sharding tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
